Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Identified Anomalies 1 R to P Receive to Approve Average of 25 hours with extremely low standard deviation 3600 seconds 1 hour suggests a rigid approval schedule rather than natural process variation 2 P to N Approve to Notify Average 7 day delay 604800 seconds with high standard deviation 172800 seconds 2 days indicates inconsistent notification practices possibly due to backlogs or resource constraints 3 A to C Assign to Close 2 hour average with 1 hour standard deviation is unusually fast for a process that should involve evaluation and approval steps suggesting premature closures 4 E to N Evaluate to Notify Only 5 minutes average with 1 minute standard deviation is unrealistically quick potentially indicating skipped steps or automation errors 5 R to E Receive to Evaluate 1 day average with 8 hour standard deviation may be too short given the complexity of insurance evaluation processes

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypotheses on Anomaly Causes 1 Rigid Approval Schedule R to P Fixed daily approval batch processing creating artificial timing patterns System enforced deadlines rather than natural business flow Possible data entry errors causing timestamp inconsistencies 2 Delayed Notifications P to N Manual notification process creating bottlenecks Resource constraints in customer service teams System integration issues between approval and notification systems Selective notification practices for certain claim types 3 Premature Closures A to C Adjusters closing claims without proper evaluation System errors closing claims automatically Fraudulent or test claims in the system Inadequate workflow enforcement 4 Rapid Evaluation Notification Transitions E to N Automated notifications immediately after evaluation System errors skipping notification step Test data or system generated events Different workflow for specific claim types 5 Short Receive Evaluate Times Insufficient time for proper claim assessment Complex claims being marked as simple Geographic or specialization bottlenecks

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Fixed daily approval batch processing creating artificial timing patterns System enforced deadlines rather than natural business flow Possible data entry errors causing timestamp inconsistencies Manual notification process creating bottlenecks Resource constraints in customer service teams System integration issues between approval and notification systems Selective notification practices for certain claim types Adjusters closing claims without proper evaluation System errors closing claims automatically Fraudulent or test claims in the system Inadequate workflow enforcement Automated notifications immediately after evaluation System errors skipping notification step Test data or system generated events Different workflow for specific claim types Insufficient time for proper claim assessment Complex claims being marked as simple Geographic or specialization bottlenecks

Name: Validation - PE
Text: SQL Verification Queries 1 Identify claims with unusually fast R to P transitions 2 Find claims with extremely long P to N delays 3 Identify claims closed very quickly after assignment 4 Check for claims missing expected activities in sequence 5 Correlate anomalies with adjusters and regions 6 Examine E to N transitions for anomalies 7 Analyze claim types with timing anomalies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Check for claims missing expected activities in sequence Should have at least 6 activities Assign followed immediately by Close Approved but not notified

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Where extract epoch from approve time minus receive time is less than 7200 More than 14 days Less than 2 hours Very low variability Less than 5 minutes

Name: Validation - NE
Text: R to E Receive to Evaluate 1 day average with 8 hour standard deviation may be too short given the complexity of insurance evaluation processes

Name: Validation - NE
Text: String agg ce activity ordered by ce timestamp like A C Assign followed immediately by Close Or string agg ce activity ordered by ce timestamp like P N is null Approved but not notified
